entries.title: OpenAI’s Impact on Users Facing State AG Scrutiny
entries.summary: A group of state attorneys general has reportedly launched an investigation into OpenAI. The artificial intelligence startup was served with a subpoena Friday (June 12) seeking information on an array of its activities and user impact, The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported, citing sources familiar with the matter. entries.title: Key Non-Negotiables for a Controllable AI Adoption in Banking
entries.summary: In the FinextraTV studio at The Temenos Community Forum in Copenhagen, Sai Rangachari, Chief Product Officer, Temenos, discussed the balance of retaining control, whilst embedding AI into banking processes. entries.title: AI Can Mimic Bloomberg. Replacing the Terminal Is Another Matter.
entries.summary: “A 24‑hour blackout would honestly feel like losing a limb for most people on a desk,” says Tom Banfield, Head of Financial Derivatives at institutional broker Britannia Global Markets. This is the peculiar, symbiotic reality of the financial professional with the Bloomberg Terminal. entries.title: Deepfakes Leave Digital Forensics Expert Doubting His Abilities
entries.summary: Hany Farid has long been the world’s foremost expert in digital forensics. Now, the rise of artificial intelligence deepfakes has left him doubting his abilities, according to a New York Times (NYT) profile published Sunday (June 14). Farid, the report said, has spent years helping government, law enforcement, journalists and human rights groups determine […] The post Deepfakes Leave Digital

entries.title: Anthropic Suspends Mythos Access Following Government Security Concerns
entries.summary: Anthropic has disabled some access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models. The move came Friday (June 12) in response to a U.S. government export control directive which cited unspecified “national security authorities,” the company wrote in its announcement. It added that the directive called on Anthropic to suspend access to those […] The post Anthropic Suspends Mythos Access Following Gove

entries.title: China Takes on Swift With New Cross-Border Payment System
entries.summary: China is reportedly preparing the commercial launch of a digital currency-focused cross-border payment program. The new platform, dubbed “mBridge,” is being backed by the central banks of China, Hong Kong, Saudi Arabia, Thailand and the United Arab Emirates, the Financial Times (FT) reported Saturday (June 13), citing sources familiar with the matter. entries.title: Investor Pointe launches, designed to help private markets firms serve more investors
entries.summary: Investor Pointe launches today as a technology and services company built to unify how private markets firms operate. The company unites an AI-enabled platform, a specialist services team, and a portfolio of tailored solutions to support private markets firms throughout the entire investor lifecycle, from fund design and fundraising through to investor servicing, reporting, and data optimisation.

entries.title: SpaceX IPO: UK retail investors boost account funding by 27%, TrueLayer data shows
entries.summary: TrueLayer has recorded a sharp rise in Pay by Bank transactions into trading and investment accounts ahead of the SpaceX listing, expected to be one of the largest public offerings and notably includes a significant retail allocation.

entries.title: EBAday 2026: EBA CLEARING and Swift CEOs to outline the future of payments
entries.summary: EBAday will host a high-level CEO fireside chat featuring Hays Littlejohn, CEO of EBA CLEARING, and Javier Pérez-Tasso, CEO of Swift, on 16 June at the Bella Center in Copenhagen, Denmark – a country consistently ranked among Europe’s most digitally advanced societies.

entries.title: Why Core Modernisation Can Never Be Fully Complete
entries.summary: Joining FinextraTV at the Temenos Community Forum, Rohit Chauhan, Chief Technology Officer, Temenos discussed the impact of composability on the ability to overcome technical debt. He explained the benefit of a gradual core modernisation, using composable modules, compared with standard, more aggressive approaches, enabling the 'blast radius' of the problem to feel smaller. entries.title: How Banks Are Transforming Their Business Through SaaS
entries.summary: Talking to FinextraTV at the Temenos Community Forum in Copenhagen, Edgardo Torres Caballero, SVP, Global Partnerships, Temenos, and Yves Dupuy, Global Leader, Financial Services Solutions, AWS, shared their thoughts on how cloud infrastructure deployment is progressing. entries.title: The Six Most Significant Challenges in European Payments Today
entries.summary: Joining the FinextraTV studio at Diebold Nixdorf's Intersect event in Cannes, Loys Moulin, Projects and Marketing Director, Cartes Bancaires, provides his insight into the six most significant challenges within European payments. Addressing the position of cash - and the cashless society as a myth - Moulin positioned Europe as a key player in cash significance, sovereignty, and resilience. He disc

entries.title: Finseta Swings to Full-Year Loss as Expansion Costs Outrun Revenue Growth
entries.summary: Finseta fell into a loss in 2025 as the cost of expanding into Dubai, Canada and corporate banking outran a 9% rise in revenue, ending a run of profitability for the foreign exchange and payments firm. The AIM-listed company (AIM: FIN ), formerly known as Cornerstone FS, reported a net loss of £1.1 million for the year ended December 31, reversing a £1.0 million profit in 2024. Adjusted EBITDA, th

entries.title: Robinhood Buys Regulatory Foothold in Canada With WonderFi Acquisition
entries.summary: Robinhood has officially entered the Canadian market after closing its acquisition of crypto platform WonderFi. The company gains immediate access to local licenses, infrastructure, and an established customer base. The deal highlights a growing preference for buying regulated market access rather than building it from scratch. For firms expanding across multiple jurisdictions, acquiring an existi

entries.title: Wintermute Becomes Latest Market Maker to Enter Prediction Markets
entries.summary: Wintermute Trading confirmed it is now providing continuous, two-sided liquidity across major prediction market platformsю This is the latest institutional market maker to move into the rapidly scaling event-contracts sector. The firm processes more than $3.5 trillion in annual trading volume across over 70 exchanges. It is now providing liquidity on Kalshi and Polymarket, extending the same tradi

entries.title: Wise Under Belgian Investigation Over €500 Million in Suspicious Transactions
entries.summary: Money transfer firm Wise is under investigation by prosecutors in Belgium over concerns that its accounts were used to launder the proceeds of fraud, corruption and drug trafficking, the company confirmed today (Monday) after the inquiry was first reported by The Bureau of Investigative Journalism . The case centers on about €500 million in suspicious transactions. Wise accounts had turned up in h

entries.title: APP fraud back on the rise as crooks stole £1.3bn in 2025 - UK Finance
entries.summary: Criminals stole £1.28 billion through fraud in the UK last year, up four per cent on 2024, with authorised push payment (APP) fraud rising sharply following a drop off the previous year.

entries.title: Current raises $80m
entries.summary: Current has raised $80 million in a Series E equity financing round that values the US personal finance app at $1.5 billion.
